Blog

Essays, interviews and reviews about all things hybrid arts.

March 9, 2026

the only thing I've done is I'm an artist figuring out what an artist is

Everything I've done, the only thing I've done is I'm an artist figuring out what an artist is. What is the game we play? What do we do that's special, right now?

November 16, 2025

Alone You're Left With Just an Idea: Interview with Wafaa Bilal

People think we have courage, but actually we simply don't know how to live with fear. … But we are afraid. We are terrified. We are the canaries in the coal mines: hey, there's danger, you got to be alert. And the powers that be don't know what to do with us.

August 6, 2025

Science Gallery: The Dating Agency for Collaborations

We'd rather be where teenagers come on a first date than a quiet exhibition space. It's all about the conversation.

March 12, 2025

Culture is Not an Industry: Interview with Justin O’Connor

A thriving cultural life makes us better citizens. Not necessarily more productive citizens in the sense of producing more stuff, but flourishing citizens, as individuals and communities. That's the value of arts and culture for public policy.

January 31, 2025

The strangeness of what's happening: Interview with Oron Catts

Art is a very important instrument for highlighting issues that need more scrutiny, and for making what's hidden and obscured meaningful.

December 13, 2024

The Art of Talking to Each Other: Interview with Shanai Matteson

Our work for recognition and justice will have more impact if it grows from real relationships, rather than ideological position.&nbsp;

November 15, 2024

Funding Hybrid Arts. Investing in Hybrid Arts, pt. IV: Material Support

Material Support for Hybrid Arts: revisiting the report “Investing in Creativity” through the lens of Hybrid Arts.

October 30, 2024

On Arts, AI and Human Rights: Interview with Caroline Sinders

I'm always thinking about how art can be useful in a practical sense. How can we use creativity to galvanize people towards action? That's the real power of this work - it can touch people emotionally through artistic elements while also informing them through research.&nbsp;

October 6, 2024

What Are Artists Selling? Investing in Hybrid Arts, pt. III: Demand / Markets

Revisiting the report “Investing in Creativity” through the lens of Hybrid Arts: Demand/Markets

September 23, 2024

Art Is a Right: Review of the Book 'Culture Is Not an Industry'

Economics … simply cannot deal with the future. But culture can.

September 3, 2024

What’s Good? Investing in Hybrid Arts, pt. II: Validation

Revisiting the report “Investing in Creativity” through the lens of Hybrid Arts: Valdation
